name: KBGAN
description: KBGAN is an adversarial learning framework designed to improve the performance of existing knowledge graph embedding models. It addresses the challenge of generating useful negative training examples, which is difficult because knowledge graphs typically only contain positive facts. Inspired by generative adversarial networks (GANs), KBGAN uses one knowledge graph embedding model to act as a negative sample generator and another desired model to function as the discriminator during training. A key feature of this framework is its independence from the concrete form of both the generator and the discriminator, allowing it to utilize a wide variety of knowledge graph embedding models as building blocks. In experiments, KBGAN was used to adversarially train translation-based models (TRANSE and TRANSD) using probability-based models (DISTMULT and COMPLEX). The framework improves target embedding model performance substantially under various settings when evaluated on the link prediction task.
